lessons.title: C++ Introduction
lessons.content:
What is C++?

  • C++ is a cross-platform language that can be used to create high-performance applications.
  • C++ was developed by Bjarne Stroustrup, as an extension to the C language.
  • C++ gives programmers a high level of control over system resources and memory.

lessons.title: C++ Get Started
lessons.content:
Quickstart

The following C++ code prints "Hello World" to the screen:

#include <iostream> using namespace std; int main() {   cout << "Hello World!";   return 0; }

lessons.title: C++ Syntax
lessons.content:
Syntax Breakdown

  • #include <iostream> is a header file library that lets us work with input and output objects.
  • using namespace std means that we can use names for objects and variables from the standard library.
  • int main() is the main function where the execution of code begins.
  • cout is an object used together with the insertion operator (<<) to output/print text.

lessons.title: C++ Output
lessons.content:
Output / Printing

The cout object, together with the << operator, is used to output values/print text:

cout << "Hello World!";

You can add as many << operators as you want. However, note that it does not insert a new line at the end of the output.

To insert a new line, you can use the character or the endl manipulator.

lessons.title: C++ Comments
lessons.content:
Comments

Single-line comments start with two forward slashes (//).

Multi-line comments start with /* and end with */.

lessons.title: C++ Variables
lessons.content:
Variables

  • int - stores integers
  • double - stores floating point numbers
  • char - stores single characters
  • string - stores text
  • bool - stores booleans
